era: 2008-2016. Hip-Hop/R&B/Neo-Soul/Afrofuturism. E=5 -- genre-bending Black femme artistry. Sub Pop. Connected to Shabazz Palaces (degree 103). Central District. Stasia Irons (MC) + Catherine Harris-White (vocalist). Sub Pop hip-hop expansion. Genre dissolution. Thirteenth degree in hip-hop era (134-146).

location.formative: Both members Seattle-based. Central District creative community. Connected to Shabazz Palaces and broader Black art scene. Active during peak CD gentrification period.
location.career: THEESatisfaction. Genre-bending duo. Two albums on Sub Pop: awE naturalE (2012), EarthEE (2015). Hiatus ~2016. Solo work: SassyBlack (Stasia), Catherine Harris-White.

members.name: Stasia Irons (SassyBlack)
members.note: Rhythmic vocal delivery. Hip-hop dimension. Solo: SassyBlack (electronic, experimental).

members.name: Catherine Harris-White
members.note: R&B, neo-soul, harmonies. Visual art. Melodic dimension. Emotional texture.

associated_musicians.name: Shabazz Palaces
associated_musicians.note: Ishmael Butler + Tendai Maraire. Frequent collaboration, touring together, shared Central District creative ecosystem. 43-degree arc (103-146).

associated_musicians.name: Ishmael Butler
associated_musicians.note: Appeared on THEESatisfaction records. Shabazz Palaces (103), Digable Planets (144). Butler's network spans 103-144-146.

associated_musicians.name: Sub Pop Records
associated_musicians.note: Historically indie rock/grunge. Hip-hop expansion with Shabazz Palaces (103) and THEESatisfaction (146).

theory_nodes.concept: Genre dissolution -- boundaries between hip-hop, R&B, neo-soul, electronic, and psychedelia cease to exist; EarthEE IS the dissolution of genre; genre labels become useless
examples: awE naturalE (2012): collage of hip-hop, R&B, psychedelic, ambient.
Tracks shift between hip-hop, house, ambient, soul, noise.
theory_nodes.description: Genre dissolution. Boundaries erased. All genres simultaneously.

ancestors: Jazz Rap (144) -- fusion of two genres.

theory_nodes.concept: Sub Pop hip-hop expansion -- second hip-hop/R&B act on Sub Pop after Shabazz Palaces (103); historically indie rock label expands identity; genuine aesthetic evolution, not cynical diversification
examples: Shabazz Palaces (103): first Sub Pop hip-hop act in engine.
Sub Pop's tradition of signing boundary-pushers continues.
Central District creative ecosystem within Sub Pop.
theory_nodes.description: Sub Pop hip-hop expansion. Grunge label evolves.

genealogy.relationship: THEESatisfaction at 146. Sub Pop expansion confirmed.

theory_nodes.concept: Black femme artistry -- two Black women in predominantly white, male Seattle scene; unapologetically femme; art grounded in Black femme experience; joy, struggle, sexuality, community, resistance
examples: Stasia + Cat: two Black women creating on their own terms.
Solo work continues the artistic vision independently.
theory_nodes.description: Black femme artistry. Unapologetic. Joyful resistance.

cross_references.relationship: SUB POP ECOSYSTEM! Collaborators, tour partners, Central District. Shabazz (dark, cosmic, abstract) + THEESatisfaction (warm, communal, joyful). 43-degree arc (103-146). Same label, same neighborhood, opposite affect.
BUTLER CONNECTION! Ishmael Butler (Shabazz Palaces, 103; Digable Planets, 144) collaborated with THEESatisfaction. Butler's network: 103-144-146.
PREVIOUS DEGREE! Conscious duo rap (145) to genre-dissolving duo (146). Both duos. Both Central District-adjacent.
CENTRAL DISTRICT! CD hip-hop continues. Producer/DJ (138) to genre-bending duo (146). The Central District's range.
